title: Today's AI Brief: Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
summary: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to diagnose rare diseases, and OpenAI shares guidance on third-party AI evaluations.

body.text: I'm catching up on some interesting developments in the world of AI. It seems that AI is making a real difference in healthcare, which is great to see. Boston Children’s Hospital is using OpenAI technology to improve patient care and help diagnose rare diseases. I think it's awesome when technology can help doctors solve puzzles that have been lingering for a while.

According to OpenAI, Boston Children’s Hospital has used their technology to diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases. This is a significant step forward in the adoption of AI in healthcare. It's not just about solving individual cases, but also about reducing the operational burden on healthcare professionals, which can be overwhelming. I'm guessing this technology can help doctors focus on what they do best – taking care of patients.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
